items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Core line matches the approved translation and preserves the mock-heroic register (“Vague doubts haunt me once again...”), which is the main comedic engine. However, the actual subtitle repeats the whole clause twice (once as 569 and again as 570), which is not in the source and reads like a duplication artifact. Also, the reference analysis notes that «У Шпака — ... у посла — ...» implies loss/association rather than simple possession; the approved English keeps the elliptical “X’s Y, Z’s W” which can still be read as possession, but since it is identical to the reference, it is acceptable as precedent.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; it preserves the repetitive, banal Soviet-polite self-introduction that creates the era-clash humor.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; keeps the sudden authoritarian, pompous demand that is funny coming from Bunsha.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; retains the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern setting.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; preserves the mistaken supernatural framing and keeps “demons” as the agent, matching the reference note.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; keeps the mock-biblical proclamation and the religious punch in an atheist Soviet context.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; captures the MC/announcer vibe and the release-into-chaos effect.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and the comic understatement of criminal luck.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preventing confusion and preserving the Soviet-satire inversion.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation; preserves the comic rhythm, the escalating inventory, and the self-incriminating counting.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation:
And they’ll cure you!
And they’ll cure you too!
And they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Semantically and stylistically aligned with the approved translation: calm, sing-song resignation that turns the psychiatric threat into a shared absurdist joke. Minor punctuation/line-break differences don’t harm the effect.

summary.interpretation: 0.45 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.09 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
